articles.title: How Spoor uses AI to track birds and improve wind farm planning
articles.description: Our exclusive interview with Spoor CEO Ask Helseth
articles.content: One of the biggest hurdles facing new wind farms is proving how wildlife interacts with turbines in the real world. Developers and regulators need to understand how wildlife interacts with wind turbines, but much of the current process relies on inte... [9189 chars]

articles.title: Single vote separates Nederland mayoral candidates
articles.description: The CEO of an AI company who helped lead the charge in Nederland's purchase of Eldora the incumbent mayor.
articles.content:
The CEO of an AI company who helped lead the charge in Nederland’s purchase of Eldora Mountain Resort leads the race for the town’s mayoral seat by a single vote.
Mayor Pro Tem Nichole Sterling has a 261-260 vote edge over incumbent Billy Giblin. The... [2199 chars]

articles.title: ‘No such thing as absolute safety’: Japan embraces nuclear post-Fukushima
articles.description: The country is rapidly moving to restart reactors as artificial intelligence increases electricity demand and foreign wars choke gas supplies.
articles.content:
This week, Japan will open its 16th reactor since the 2011 Fukushima nuclear accident — at a nuclear plant run by the same utility that oversaw Daiichi during the meltdown.
Toyoshi Fuketa, former chair of Japan’s Nuclear Regulation Authority, said th... [11644 chars]

articles.title: Zoom will now check if you are a human or an AI imposter during video meetings
articles.description: Zoom has launched human verification in video meetings, partnering with Sam Altman's World to display a "Verified Human" badge for participants whose identities pass a three-step biometric check.
articles.content: Zoom video calls just got a new kind of awkward small feature. The platform will now ask you whether you’re human. It has partnered with World, Sam Altman’s iris-scanning identity company (previously known as Worldcoin), to add real-time human verifi... [1856 chars]

articles.title: US man, 36, dies by suicide after AI chat suggested ‘joining’ it in digital world: ‘I am scared to die'
articles.description: A Florida man’s death by suicide has triggered global debate on the psychological risks posed by increasingly human-like AI chatbots. Chat records cited in a lawsuit suggest the bot may have reinforced delusions, raising urgent questions about safety, regulation, and emotional dependency on AI.
articles.content:
A 36-year-old man in Florida died by suicide after weeks of extensive interaction with an artificial intelligence chatbot, a case that is now at the centre of a lawsuit against Google and a wider debate on AI safety.
Jonathan Gavalas, described by hi... [3101 chars]

articles.title: Iran’s AI slopaganda: who is behind the giant billboards in Tehran?
articles.description: Three-storey images of Khamenei, Khomeini and Hormuz are being erected at ever greater speed. The timing is no accident
articles.content:
Like many other residents of Tehran, Sohrab* is used to seeing a new face staring down at him: AI-generated portraits of Mojtaba Khamenei, the new supreme leader.
“They’ve made the city ugly,” the 35-year-old restaurant manager said. “Everywhere, the... [6441 chars]

articles.title: All Gemini users can now access Notebook projects on the web without paying a dime
articles.description: Gemini now lets all free users create Notebooks on the web, a shared workspace that syncs with NotebookLM. You can store files, past chats, and sources in one place for smarter AI responses.
articles.content: Google just made one of Gemini’s most useful features available to everyone. The Notebooks feature, initially rolled out to paid AI subscribers earlier this month, is now available to all free users on the web. If you use Gemini regularly, this is a ... [1753 chars]

articles.title: 'Unbelievable' fraudsters in bear costumes caught trying to fake damage to a Rolls-Royce
articles.description: People have been jailed after allegedly using a bear suit to fake “wild animal” damage to a £320,000 Rolls-Royce and two Mercedes, as UK insurers warn AI is fuelling a surge in “digital” fraud

articles.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
articles.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.
articles.content:
Cerebras Systems, a startup building what CEO Andrew Feldman describes as “the fastest AI hardware for training and inference,” has filed to go public.
The company previously filed for an initial public offering in 2024, but that was delayed due to a... [871 chars]

articles.title: Here is how crypto firms are adapting as AI is increasingly eating into venture capital fundings
articles.description: AI companies raised $242 billion (80% of global venture funding) in early 2026, with Gartner projecting total AI spending will reach $2.52 trillion this year.
articles.content:
Forty cents of every venture capital dollar invested in crypto companies in 2025 went to firms building products that combine artificial intelligence and crypto, more than double the 18 cents a year earlier.
